2606.22249.title: On the Expressive Power of Weight Quantization in Large Language Models
2606.22249.paper_summary: In recent years, weight quantization that encodes the learnable parameters of large language models in an $n$-bit format has garnered significant attention due to its potential for model compression and inference acceleration. Many practical techniques have been developed; however, the theoretical understanding of many aspects, especially the approximation and degradation of expressive power as the number of quantization bits decreases, remains unclear. In this paper, we provide a theoretical investigation into the expressive capability of large language models relative to the number of quantization bits. We argue that 1.58-bit is the limiting precision for weight quantization by establishing the universal approximation and expressive collapse properties of weight-quantized models with respect to the number of quantization bits. Additionally, we confirm that weight quantization leads to expressive degradation, in which the expressive capacity of weight-quantized models degrades polynomially as the number of quantization bits decreases. These theoretical findings provide a solid foundation for advancing weight quantization in the context of scaling laws and shed insights for future research in model compression and inference acceleration.
2606.22249.paper_summary_zh: 近年來，將大型語言模型的可學習參數編碼為 $n$ 位格式的權重量化因其在模型壓縮和推理加速方面的潛力而受到廣泛關注。<lb/>許多實用技術已經被開發出來；然而，許多方面的理論理解，特別是當量化位數減少時的近似和表達能力的退化，仍然不清楚。<lb/>在本文中，我們對大型語言模型相對於量化位數的表達能力進行了理論研究。<lb/>我們主張，1.58 位是權重量化的限制精度，通過建立與量化位數有關的權重量化模型的通用近似和表達崩潰特性來證明這一點。<lb/>此外，我們確認權重量化導致表達退化，其中權重量化模型的表達能力隨著量化位數的減少而多項式地退化。<lb/>這些理論發現為在擴展法則背景下推進權重量化提供了堅實的基礎，並為未來在模型壓縮和推理加速方面的研究提供了見解。
2606.22249.author: Shao-Qun Zhang et.al.
